Object.Name: Timeslot
Object.Description1: Timeslot is an instance of a schedule

Item.Name: start
Item.Description: Intended start time in seconds since UNIX epoch. Could NOT be adjusted, the whole timeslot has to be cancelled and rescheduled instead. GW timestamp of the RUNNING state should be quite close to this one and should accurately describe the actual time of starting the action.

Item.Name: adjusted_stop
Item.Description: Best known stop time (from the device standpoint). Depending on the value of `state` could be either in the past or in the future. Could be changed with `timeslot.adjust_duration()` call or with an `actuator.stop()` call. GW timestamp of the state, immediately following the RUNNING, should be very close to this value.

Item.Name: initial_source
Item.Description: The reason why this timeslot was created, specifically why `start` has exactly this value. Also applies to why `adjusted_stop` has its value in case `adjustment_source` is NONE.

Item.Name: adjustment_source
Item.Description: How adjusted_stop was set. Same values as for initial_source.

Item.Name: pre_offset
Item.Description: Magic cookie used by backend. Copied over from schedule config.

Item.Name: adjust_duration
Item.Description: Adjust duration of planned or running timeslot. Only has effect on timeslots in states SCHEDULED, WILL_START, RUNNING (has no effect on timeslots in all other states). Takes two arguments: 0: integer: adjustment source. Updates the adjustment_source field. See initial_source above for list of available values. Use value 2 if not sure. 1: integer: new duration in seconds. Uses OMA format. Example usage with bnw_cli: $ poetry run bnw ipso exc "$DEVICE" timeslot/0/set_duration "as=[0='10',1='120']"
